## Phase 1 — Baseline eval

Goal: measure HF checkpoints on prod-shaped `remember_target` before training.

Suites: plan chunks, cursor-shaped assistant text, workflow procedures, technical rules, noise.

Metrics: content_grounding_rate, curriculum_bleed_rate, fail_safe_ignore_rate.

### Chunking pipeline

Split long assistant handoffs on markdown headers and numbered steps. Target 600–1200 tokens per chunk so prompt overhead fits 2048 context. Call remember() per chunk with shared source_id and :chunk-N suffix.

I updated the remember path to reject ungrounded storage and block curriculum bleed. Prod max_new_tokens is now 384 to match eval. Next step is the prod grounding harness with fixtures for plan chunks and review-pr workflow.

Root cause: the model memorized gate curriculum templates instead of extracting facts from assistant text. LoCoMo Hit@k was high while memory.content had no usable facts, so answer accuracy stayed at 0%.

# Review a pull request

1. Get PR info with `gh pr view`.
2. Check the target branch tracks the intended base.
3. List changed files with `gh pr diff --name-only`.
4. Review each changed file for correctness and scope.
5. Summarize findings and request changes or approve.

RunPod launch policy: always pass --proxy-user, use two-phase deploy then train-gate5 --pod-id, verify tmux + psm_model.train + GPU util within 15s. Never SCP to proxy; use tar-push. Export PSM_RUNPOD=1 in tmux or training runs on CPU.
Project rule: prefer explicit return types on exported functions in psm-core. Avoid default exports in library modules. Match existing import style with .js extensions in TypeScript sources.
API convention: remember() accepts llmResponse as the assistant text to extract from. userMessage is optional context only when a real user turn exists in the product path.
Okay, sure. Let me know if you need anything else.
